mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor demand and impending consumption contraction, triggering institutional risk-off rebalancing that depresses S&P 500 prices over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the negative signal, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns during recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent strength in housing starts and retail sales volume confirms broad-based consumer demand resilience, supporting corporate revenue forecasts and driving positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or quarterly forward returns show no statistical difference between high-signal and low-signal regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding its long-term trend indicates supply chain normalization and capacity utilization gains, which directly supports corporate margin expansion and yields positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain flat or negative when INDPRO sustains values above 100 for consecutive months, indicating the signal loses predictive power during supply booms.

mechanism_narrative: When headline inflation consistently outpaces the federal funds rate by more than 1.5 percentage points, real borrowing costs turn deeply positive, compressing equity valuation multiples and forcing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns do not decline when the real rate spread exceeds 2%, or the interaction term shows zero statistical significance in a multivariate regression over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Steady month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls sustains wage-driven consumer spending, providing a reliable earnings floor that generates positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly investment horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns show no correlation with payroll growth trends, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark during periods of payroll deceleration.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth coupled with stable consumer prices indicates healthy demand-pull expansion without inflationary overheating, which optimally supports equity risk premiums and drives positive monthly returns.
failure_condition: The interaction signal produces negative predictive power when GDP growth exceeds 3% annually, or forward returns fail to differentiate between high-growth/low-inflation and low-growth/high-inflation regimes.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in retail sales volume triggers immediate analyst earnings downgrade expectations, causing short-term equity repricing and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive despite consecutive retail sales declines, or the signal fails to predict drawdowns during seasonal adjustment periods.
